items.title: Solana Adds Machine Payments Protocol for Stablecoin APIs
items.author: @solana

whoToFollow.reason: Key partner in Machine Payments Protocol integration

content.signal: Solana now supports the Machine Payments Protocol, enabling API-based stablecoin payments via Stripe and Tempo.
content.story:
Solana has integrated the Machine Payments Protocol (MPP), developed in collaboration with Stripe and Tempo, into its ecosystem. This move allows developers to use the @solana/mpp SDK to accept any stablecoin on Solana, including both SPL and Token2022 formats, through API-driven payments. For projects already leveraging MPP, Solana now acts as a direct payment rail, expanding their options for programmable, agentic payments.

### What's Happening
Solana’s latest integration brings Stripe and Tempo’s MPP into the fold, making it easier for developers to build APIs that accept stablecoin payments natively on Solana. The SDK abstracts away much of the complexity, handling compliance, wallet management, and payment routing. This is part of a broader push to position Solana as a go-to platform for enterprise-grade financial infrastructure, with a focus on automation and machine-to-machine payments.

Recent ecosystem updates reinforce this direction: Solana launched its new Developer Platform, targeting enterprises looking to launch financial products quickly. Partnerships with major payment and financial companies like Mastercard, Worldpay, and Western Union further underline Solana’s ambition to become a core layer for digital payments and tokenized assets.

### Why It Matters
The addition of MPP support is a significant step for Solana’s enterprise and developer adoption. By enabling seamless, programmable payments with stablecoins, Solana lowers the barrier for fintechs and businesses to build on-chain financial services. The ability to handle compliance, wallets, and fiat ramps in one stack is attractive for companies seeking to automate payments or launch new products without deep blockchain expertise.

This move also strengthens Solana’s position in the competitive landscape of blockchain payments. With Stripe’s involvement, there’s potential for broader merchant and developer adoption, especially as stablecoins and tokenization become central to new financial applications. The focus on agentic, API-driven payments aligns with trends toward automation and embedded finance.

### The Risk or Debate
While the integration is technically promising, it introduces new risks. Relying on third-party protocols and SDKs (like MPP) can create dependencies and potential attack surfaces, especially as payment flows become more automated. Compliance and regulatory requirements for stablecoin payments remain a moving target, and any missteps could impact both users and builders.

There’s also the question of adoption: while Solana is positioning itself as enterprise-ready, it remains to be seen how quickly traditional businesses and fintechs will trust and integrate these new rails. The ecosystem’s rapid pace of innovation can be a double-edged sword, as security and compliance must keep up with new features.

### What To Watch
Key signals to monitor include the pace of developer adoption of the @solana/mpp SDK, announcements of new enterprise integrations, and any updates from Stripe or Tempo on their roadmap. Watch for regulatory developments around stablecoin payments, as these could affect how MPP and similar protocols are used on Solana. Finally, keep an eye on how Solana’s broader enterprise push—especially partnerships with payment giants—translates into real-world usage and transaction volume.
takeaways: Solana now supports the Machine Payments Protocol for API-based stablecoin payments.
The integration aims to simplify compliance, wallet management, and payment routing for developers.
Risks include new dependencies, regulatory uncertainty, and the challenge of driving enterprise adoption.

whoToFollow.reason: Builder of the AI agent and related LP tools
Shares insights on top-performing LP pools and ecosystem trends

content.signal: A Solana-based AI agent for liquidity pools is being widely tested, showing strong user interest and real earnings.
content.story:
A Solana builder recently opened access to an AI agent that automates liquidity provision, allowing nearly 100 users to test it for free. This agent, which has generated over $189 in 11 days and up to $101 in a single day, is designed to optimize returns from providing liquidity on Meteora. The builder, who started with no coding experience, developed a suite of supporting tools, including transaction parsers and investment dashboards, before launching the agent.

What's Happening
The AI agent is being distributed to a new cohort of 50 testers after initial positive results. It automates LP strategies on Meteora, a Solana-based liquidity platform, and has demonstrated consistent daily profits with only one losing day in the reported period. The builder’s journey highlights a rapid learning curve and the creation of multiple tools to support LP activities, reflecting a hands-on approach to solving real user problems.

Meteora itself is seeing increased activity, with top-performing pools attracting more liquidity providers. The platform has also rolled out mobile UI upgrades, making it easier for users to interact with pools and manage positions on the go. This aligns with the broader trend of integrating AI and automation into DeFi workflows, lowering barriers for non-technical users.

Why It Matters
The adoption of AI agents in DeFi represents a shift toward more accessible and efficient liquidity management. By automating complex strategies, these tools can help users optimize returns without constant manual intervention. The builder’s story also signals that technical barriers are falling, as individuals with little coding background can now create impactful tools and share them with the community.

Meteora’s growing ecosystem and improved user interfaces suggest that Solana DeFi is becoming more user-friendly and competitive. The influx of new LPs and builders experimenting with AI-driven tools could accelerate innovation and liquidity depth across Solana’s protocols. This momentum may attract both new users and developers, reinforcing Solana’s position in the DeFi landscape.

The Risk or Debate
Automated agents introduce new risks, including potential bugs, strategy failures, or unforeseen market conditions that could lead to losses. Relying on third-party tools also raises questions about transparency and trust, especially when strategies are not fully open-sourced. The rapid onboarding of non-technical users may lead to misunderstandings about how these agents operate or the risks involved in LP activities.

What To Watch
Further adoption metrics from the current cohort of testers will reveal whether the agent’s performance holds up at scale. Updates from Meteora on pool performance and additional UI improvements could influence user retention and growth. The emergence of similar AI-driven tools across other Solana protocols may signal a broader trend toward automation in DeFi. Ongoing feedback from testers and builders will shape the next generation of LP agents and supporting infrastructure.
takeaways: A Solana AI agent for liquidity provision is being tested by over 100 users.
The agent generated over $189 in 11 days, with mostly positive daily returns.
Meteora is enhancing its platform with mobile UI upgrades and attracting more LPs.

whoToFollow.reason: Provides detailed breakdowns and critical analysis of Backpack's airdrop and tokenomics.
Tracks real-time Solana NFT and token market movements and community sentiment.

content.signal: Backpack’s $BP token airdrop has triggered mixed reactions, with long-term Mad Lads supporters questioning fairness and value distribution.
content.story:
Backpack’s highly anticipated $BP token airdrop is live, but instead of celebration, it’s stirring debate across the Solana ecosystem. Long-time Mad Lads and Backpack supporters are voicing frustration over how rewards were allocated, especially after years of engagement. The airdrop’s structure and the resulting NFT price drop have put the project’s loyalty narrative under the microscope.

### What's Happening

Backpack, the team behind the Mad Lads NFT collection, has launched its $BP token airdrop. The event is notable for its scale—one of the largest on Solana—and for its promise of no insider, VC, or team allocations at launch. Only users are receiving tokens initially. The tokenomics split 25% of supply at TGE (including 1% for Mad Lads), with the rest unlocking through achievements or a post-IPO schedule.

Despite years of community support, some Mad Lads holders are questioning the distribution. Holders who staked since mint received the same allocation as those who joined just months ago, raising concerns about whether loyalty was truly rewarded. Meanwhile, Mad Lads NFTs have dropped over 40% in floor price following the airdrop, signaling disappointment among holders.

### Why It Matters

This airdrop is a major test for how NFT communities and token launches intersect. Backpack’s approach—excluding insiders and VCs—was designed to empower users, but the execution has left some core supporters feeling sidelined. The fact that Mad Lads generated $14.4M in royalties, while only $3M in tokens were allocated to holders at TGE, has fueled the debate.

The $BP token is already pre-trading at a $300–400M fully diluted valuation, putting a tangible price on Backpack points and Mad Lads NFTs. This sets a precedent for how value is distributed in future NFT-linked token launches. The community’s reaction could influence how other projects structure their airdrops and reward mechanisms.

### The Risk or Debate

The main risk is a loss of trust among the most dedicated users. If long-term supporters feel their loyalty isn’t valued, it could weaken the project’s core community. The sharp drop in Mad Lads NFT prices reflects this sentiment. There’s also uncertainty around how the remaining tokens—unlocked through achievements or post-IPO—will be distributed and whether this will address current frustrations or deepen them.

Another debate centers on fairness: Should airdrops reward longevity, activity, or simply holding? Backpack’s approach has reignited this question, and the answer will shape both user behavior and project reputations across Solana.

### What To Watch

The next few weeks will reveal whether $BP’s community-first approach can repair trust and stabilize the Mad Lads ecosystem. Watch for updates on achievement-based unlocks and how the team responds to feedback. Price action on both $BP and Mad Lads NFTs will be a key signal of sentiment.

Other Solana projects are likely to study Backpack’s experience closely. Any adjustments to future airdrop strategies—especially around rewarding true loyalty—could set new standards for the ecosystem.
takeaways: Backpack’s $BP airdrop allocated tokens only to users, with no insider or VC allocations.
Long-term Mad Lads supporters are frustrated by equal rewards for recent and early holders.
Mad Lads NFT prices dropped over 40% after the airdrop, reflecting community disappointment.
